items.title: Live: NDIS reforms needed to secure 'enormous human rights achievement', Butler says
items.description: The overhaul will see more than 160,000 kicked off the scheme by the end of the decade, reducing its projected growth from 10 per cent a year to 2 per cent — a cut in real terms.

items.title: Explainer-How Deutsche Telecom and T-Mobile US could pull off the world's biggest M&A deal
South Africa could see first winter without power cuts in years
items.description: In South Africa, the state-owned electricity company Eskom announced on Wednesday that it does not expect power outages during the winter months in the Southern Hemisphere, from April to August. This will be the first winter without power cuts since the company has been facing difficulties.

items.title: Fifty days out, who is guaranteed to make Argentina’s World Cup squad?
items.description: Coach Lionel Scaloni has the majority of his squad for the tournament in the United States, Canada and Mexico decided – a look at the problematic positions and how many spots are up for grabs. Leer más

items.title: Marsh 50 not enough as Giants fall to Archer's pace
items.description: Australia T20 skipper Mitch Marsh did his best to bail out Lucknow Super Giants, but his half-century wasn't enough to save them from defeat against Rajasthan.

items.title: Ukraine restarts Russian oil to Europe, unblocking 90-billion-euro EU loan
items.description: Resumption of oil deliveries enables Hungary to lift veto, with EU's 27 members now on track to formally approve loan.

items.title: School's out in Iran: No phone, no internet, no classes
items.description: Iran's schools are closed because of the war. Classes are being held via TV programs and the national intranet. Children without a laptop or smartphone are left out. The country is effectively cut off from the internet.

items.title: What would happen next if Iran agreed to surrender its highly enriched uranium
items.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Scott Roecker of the Nuclear Threat Initiative about how the U.S. might go about moving highly enriched uranium out of Iran.

items.title: Pope Leo criticises Equatorial Guinea prisons as he winds up Africa tour
items.description: The Pope gives a frank and passionate speech, the likes of which have characterised his tour of Africa.

items.title: China hails countries for blocking Taiwan president's flight
items.description: Taiwan's president canceled a planned trip to Eswatini after three African countries revoked permission for his plane to fly through their airspace. Beijing has denied pressuring the countries to block the flight.

items.title: Billionaire backer sues Trump family's crypto firm over alleged extortion
items.description: Billionaire investor Justin Sun is suing the family's World Liberty crypto venture after spending $45m on its tokens.

items.title: Tigray restores regional parliament, stoking fears of conflict with Addis Ababa
items.description: The Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF) announced the restoration of a regional parliament, the election of which was one of the triggers for the Tigray war, alarming observers fearing a resumption of the deadly conflict between the Ethiopian government and regional forces.

items.title: Lilies of the valley: Flowers given on International Workers' Day in France start to bloom
items.description: It is a French tradition to give friends and family lilies of the valley on Labour day, the 1st of May. And after a rainy winter, these spring flowers have come into their own earlier than usual. Our colleagues at France 2 took a look behind the scenes at lily of the valley harvest.

items.title: Trump calls for Republicans to unify around budget reconciliation push
items.description: Budget reconciliation would allow conservative lawmakers to bypass Democratic opposition in Senate with simple majority.

items.title: Iran seizes ships in Strait of Hormuz after Trump halts attacks
items.description: Iran seized two ships in the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday, April 22, tightening its grip on the strategic route.

items.title: Niger junta says it suspended nearly 3,000 NGOs in 2025
items.description: Niger's military government suspended the activities of 2,900 of the 4,700 registered local and foreign non-governmental organisations and development agencies, the interior minister said.

items.title: Israeli forces fire tear gas at funeral of Palestinians killed by settlers
items.description: Israeli forces raided the funeral of two Palestinians killed by settlers at a school in the occupied West Bank.

items.title: Milei’s auction of Argentine railroads risks sidelining US-backed bidder
items.description: The model Milei's administration has chosen for the auctioning off of Argentina’s decaying freight railways has drawn criticism from industry experts and risks sidelining one of the most prominent bidders. Leer más

items.title: Nvidia-backed VAST Data valued at $30 billion in latest funding round
South Sudan to hold elections in December as fighting rages on
items.description: After years of delays, South Sudan is expected to hold elections in December, despite the nation being on the brink of civil war.

items.title: Three ships targeted in Hormuz, Iran seizes two
items.description: Iranian forces targeted three container ships on Wednesday, seizing two, the latest incidents to threaten a crucial trade route in the Middle East war. Iran's parliament speaker said on Wednesday that the Islamic republic would not reopen the Strait of Hormuz as long as the US naval blockade remained in place, calling it a "blatant violation of the ceasefire". FRANCE 24's Reza Sayah reports from Tehran.

items.title: China condemns Japanese leaders' war shrine offerings, visits
items.description: Japan’s “negative ‌actions” concerning ‌the ⁠shrine “grossly trample on ⁠human conscience”, and China is “very indignant at these ​heinous ‌acts and strongly condemns them”, says a spokesperson for Beijing’s ‌Foreign Ministry

items.title: TSMC shows smaller, faster chips without a pricey new tool from ASML
US considers sending 1,100 Afghans to DR Congo amid resettlement halt
items.description: The Trump administration is considering a controversial new plan, to send more than 1,000 Afghans who supported US forces, not to America, but to central Africa.

items.title: Prize-winning book 'Houris' brings prison term for French-Algerian author Kamel Daoud
items.description: French-Algerian author Kamel Daoud says he has been sentenced in Algeria to three years in prison and fined over his award-winning novel “Houris,” which revisits the country’s civil war. The ruling reflects the mounting pressure on authors confronting Algeria’s violent past and raises fresh concerns over freedom of expression.

items.title: Iran: 'Going back to war is not in anyone's interest,' risk analyst says
items.description: A risk management analyst has spoken to FRANCE 24 about what it means, amid the ceasefire, if the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed. Paymon Azmoudeh is a senior consultant with the risk and compliance practice at Forward Global. He says he thinks the calculation on all sides is that going back to war is not in anyone's interest. He explained to us in Perspective what it means if the status quo continues with the ceasefire largely holding, but the economic impasse ongoing.

items.title: LIVE: Manchester City beat Burnley 1-0 – Premier League
items.description: All the updates, with post-match interviews and coverage, after our text commentary stream as City climb above Arsenal.

items.title: Did UK universities pay to ‘spy’ on pro‑Palestine students?
items.description: UK universities allegedly hired a security firm with military intelligence ties to monitor pro-Palestine students.

items.title: Japan: South Korean arrested outside Yasukuni Shrine ceremony for war dead
items.description: Police said a South Korean national holding a banner with political messages was arrested at Tokyo's Yasukuni Shrine to Japan's war dead. Japan's prime minister did not attend proceedings, but around 120 lawmakers did.

items.title: Gaming industry could unlock $22 billion in profits on AI-driven cost cuts, says Morgan Stanley
Ukraine has asked Turkiye to host a Zelenskiy-Putin meeting, Foreign Minister says
items.description: “We asked the Turks about it, we asked some ‌other capitals,” says Foreign Minister Andrii Sybiha

items.description: Zia’s passing leaves huge void, says PM

items.title: Four dead in Russia, Ukraine after exchange of drone strikes
items.description: As diplomatic talks have stalled and neither side has made a breakthrough on the front, Moscow and Kyiv have both stepped up their long-range drone attacks, firing swarms of the unmanned explosive-packed devices at each other on a nightly basis

items.title: Luis Puenzo, director of Argentina’s first Oscar-winning feature, dies at 80
items.description: Luis Puenzo, director who won Argentina’s first Oscar, has died aged 80; 'La historia oficial,' which tackled kidnapping of infants during dictatorship era, won the Academy Award for Best Foreign Language Film in 1986. Leer más

items.title: Egypt's President el-Sissi hosts Finnish leader Stubb for talks in Cairo
items.description: Egypt's President Abdel Fattah el-Sissi welcomed his Finnish counterpart on Tuesday for wide-ranging talks on the rapidly changing global order.

items.title: Second French peacekeeper dies after ambush blamed on Hezbollah
items.description: A second French soldier died on Wednesday from wounds suffered in a weekend ambush against UN peacekeepers in Lebanon blamed on Hezbollah, President Emmanuel Macron said. A first soldier, staff sergeant Florian Montorio, was shot dead in the Saturday ambush, for which Hezbollah has denied responsibility. FRANCE 24's Camille Corcoran reports.

items.title: Sexual violence in the West Bank emerges as tool to intimidate Palestinians
items.description: Systematic sexual violence and harassment by Israeli soldiers, settlers displacing Palestinians in occupied West Bank.

items.title: EU may let startups claim innovation benefits in M&A if Big Tech not involved
Iran Foreign Ministry condemns arrests in UAE as 'baseless'
items.description: Iran accuses Gulf states of allowing U.S. forces to carry out attacks from their territory, and launched missile and drone strikes against what it calls U.S. interests in those countries throughout the war

items.title: 'Very short on facts': Electoral Commission's bizarre communications in a crisis
items.description: As trust in their ability to effectively administer an election erodes by the day, so too seemingly does their transparency with the public.

items.title: Russia to block Kazakh oil flows on key pipeline supplying Berlin
items.description: Germany has confirmed that Russia will block flows of Kazakh oil to a vital refinery that supplies much of Berlin's energy needs from May 1.

items.title: Trump extends Iran ceasefire, yet blockade enforcement persists
items.description: President Donald Trump has announced the extension of a fragile ceasefire with Iran, citing a request from Pakistan and saying he is awaiting what he called a “unified proposal” from Tehran.

items.title: Harvey Weinstein’s rape retrial opens in New York for third time
items.description: Weinstein lawyer Jacob Kaplan countered that the case actually “is about consent, about choice and about regret”, echoing Weinstein’s long-time defence that his accuser has recast a willing encounter as a crime

items.title: 'Trump's high-stakes political theatre: US winning conventional war, Iran winning asymmetrical war'
items.description: Delano D'Souza is pleased to welcome Dr. William Lawrence, Senior Academic and Research Fellow-in-Residence and the Director of the North African Area Studies Program at the National Council on US-Arab Relations. As a former US diplomat in the Muslim world, he views what we are witnessing as a deliberate embrace of chaos and unpredictability as a coercive tool. Yet such theatrical instability carries diminishing returns when used as a modus operandi, explains Dr. Lawrence. "Trump thinks he can force Iran to the table by being hyperbolic, apocalyptic and chaotic. Iranians, in turn, respond with so much mockery and bravado."

items.title: Aussies crash in Madrid as Kasatkina sinks to new ranking low
items.description: Daria Kasatkina's difficult WTA Tour season has continued with an agonising defeat at the Madrid Open, as all four Aussie women crash out in the opening round.

items.title: Exclusive-Ghana directs Newmont, AngloGold, Zijin to shift mining ops to local firms by December, sources say
Will Iran war reshape global trade more than COVID?
items.description: As the Iran war simmers, trade experts wonder if this conflict will reshape supply chains more than the pandemic. Companies are already rethinking strategies to future-proof revenues from other geopolitical risks.

items.title: China pledges deeper cooperation with Mozambique
items.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping and Mozambican President Daniel Chapo have agreed to elevate ties between their countries, following talks in Beijing on Tuesday.

items.title: Can Israel’s economy sustain conflicts on multiple fronts?
items.description: The Bank of Israel’s preliminary estimates suggest that Israel’s war with Iran came at the cost of 35 billion shekels ($11.52 billion) in budgetary expenses. Its operations in Gaza till 2025 cost the country around 8.6% of its annual gross domestic product.

items.title: Stump that created hole in footpath becomes neighbourhood attraction
items.description: Adelaide residents have decorated a large tree stump and then the hole it left in a footpath to highlight the local council's delay in removing it.

items.title: Iran says ‘fully prepared’ for football team’s World Cup participation
items.description: Tehran says all necessary arrangements have been made for participation in the tournament cohosted by the US.

items.title: India bats for greater Global South representation in UNSC
items.description: At the Inter-Governmental Negotiations (IGN) meeting on UN Security Council reforms, India highlighted the “need for greater representation of the Global South, particularly in the permanent category," the Ministry of External Affairs said in a post on X.

items.title: US and Chinese envoys in Argentina clash over influence in the region
items.description: Chinese Embassy in Argentina accuses US Ambassador Peter Lamelas of “ideological prejudices” and defamation after Washington’s envoy warned about advance of Asian giant. Leer más

items.title: Microsoft to integrate Anthropic's Mythos into its security development program
REPLAY: UK Starmer takes questions in Parliament over Mandelson appointment
items.description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer was grilled during questions in Parliament over his decision to appoint Peter Mandelson as Britain's top diplomat to Washington, effectively brushing aside security concerns. Starmer faced calls to resign from the opposition, after former top civil servant Olly Robbins testified this week that there was an "atmosphere of pressure" from Downing Street to approve Mandelson's appointment so he could be in the post at the start of Donald Trump's second term in office.

items.title: South Korea says security talks with U.S. should not be linked to Coupang probe
items.description: The Ministry commented in response to a media report that Washington has ‌threatened to halt security talks unless Seoul provided legal guarantees for the safety of Coupang’s Chairman Kim Bom during a probe into the leaking ‌of customer information at the company

items.title: Maradona's daughter slams 'manipulation' of family by his doctors at trial
items.description: Gianinna Maradona alleges in court that her father's medical team engaged in “total and horrible” manipulation. Daughter of ‘El Diez’ is the first witness in second trial. Leer más

items.title: Commentary: Why emerging markets are dismissing bad Iran news
items.description: The fact that developing nations’ equities are able to rebound despite the bloc’s vulnerability to energy shocks shows there’s value in this space, says Shuli Ren for Bloomberg Opinion.

items.description: The new maps approved by the state's voters could allow Democrats to flip as many as four House seats.

items.description: Authorities say a Colombian suspected of helping to organise the killing of Miguel Uribe was captured in Buenos Aires.

items.description: The ornate Cotofenesti helmet, which was stolen in January 2025 while on loan to a Dutch museum, was recovered last month.

items.title: Record number of immigrants reside in EU in 2025
items.description: The foreign‑born population of the EU grew to around 64 million in 2025. Germany remains the biggest host of immigrants in absolute terms, but some smaller nations have a much higher percentage of foreign-born residents.

items.title: Avoid men, German police association chief tells women
items.description: Police association chief Dirk Peglow has stirred debate with his advice to women to avoid relationships with men for safety's sake. But his provocative tip is not without reason, as statistics show.

items.title: Israeli strikes kill 3 in Lebanon, Beirut to seek truce extension
items.description: Ahead of the talks on Thursday (April 23, 2026), Israel called on the Lebanese government to "work together" with it against Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah

items.description: A triple j hack investigation uncovers allegations that a booking agency has failed to pay more than 20 Australian bands and artists.

items.title: EU approves €90bn loan for Ukraine as pipeline is turned on ending deadlock
items.description: Ukraine says it has reopened the Druzhba pipeline, after months of stalemate over stalled oil supplies to neighbouring Hungary.

items.title: Iran seizes two ships in Strait of Hormuz after Trump extends ceasefire
items.description: U.K. Maritime Trade Operations Centre reported that two ships came under attack in the strait; Iran yet to officially confirm whether they would also extend the ceasefire

items.title: Ping-pong robot Ace makes history by beating top-level human players
From pop royalty to raw Americana: 'Michael', 'The New West' and 'Die, My Love'
items.description: This week's film show on arts24 spotlights a trio of strikingly different releases, led by Antoine Fuqua's highly anticipated biopic "Michael", which features a breakout performance from nephew Jaafar Jackson as the King of Pop, alongside Colman Domingo's chilling turn as patriarch Joseph Jackson.

items.title: ‘Hungry all the time’: US Navy deny food shortages after grim photos emerge
items.description: The US Navy has fiercely denied claims that its warships in the Middle East are running low on food and leaving its sailors “hungry all the time,” as Washington’s blockade in the Strait of Hormuz continues. Photos showing dismal, mostly-empty trays of food circulated online, with the claims that shortages are rife after a month and a half of war.

items.title: IBM revenue growth slows amid AI concerns; shares fall
World's worst energy crisis? Iran war sparks scramble for alternatives to Gulf oil
items.description: Will the world have enough fuel to fill the tank and fertilize the crops? The seizure of ships by Iran adding to global jitters as the extension of a ceasefire offers zero visibility on prospects for the planet’s biggest choke point for oil and gas. Nearly two months on, the Paris-based International Energy Agency is calling it the biggest-ever energy disruption in history. We’ll ask about prices at the pump, cancelled flights, and…more broadly, if oil's not flowing through the Strait of Hormuz, where to find it and what’s the alternative?

items.title: Pope Leo urges justice reform in Equatorial Guinea’s prisons
items.description: Equatorial Guinea's prison conditions face scrutiny as Pope Leo pushes for humane treatment and reforms during his visit.

items.title: Trading Day: Nasdaq hits new high

Los Angeles becomes first major US school district to limit classroom screen time
items.description: The school district, which serves about half a million students, is looking to reduce its reliance on devices after the pandemic.

items.description: Turkish president meets NATO chief as Kyiv asks Ankara to host a leaders' level meeting with Russia.

items.description: One of the pilots has been fined thousands of dollars by the military to cover repair costs.

items.description: Starmer has said he was "wrong" to appoint ‌Mandelson ⁠to the role and has expressed regret

items.title: Live: ASX to open in the red
items.description: The ASX is set to open lower after a strong finish on Wall Street. The price of oil rallies above 3 per cent. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.description: Six men pleaded not guilty to treason and terrorism charges over a botched coup plot, Nigeria media reported Wednesday, as the first prosecutions for the alleged 2025 putsch attempt get under way.

items.description: After the US president barred South Africa from attending the G20 summit at his Miami area club later this year, the French ambassador to South Africa insisted that the country should attend all G20 meetings.

items.description: Israel is 'deliberately' attacking Lebanon's water, experts say, aiming to displace or kill south Lebanon's population.

items.description: Pope Leo touched down in Equatorial Guinea on Tuesday, and was greeted by the president, scores of fans, and a red carpet welcome. It follows a three day visit to Angola.

items.description: The state government has unveiled its latest investment in CFA and forest firefighting upgrades.

items.description: The Toprain Farming Collective is calling for greater protection of strategic cropping land in central Queensland, as Comet Ridge proposes an expansion and further development of the Mahalo Gas Hub.

items.description: The park, near the venue where inconclusive Iran-U.S. peace talks took place this month, provides respite to those who visit it.

items.title: How grievance turns to violence in a mass shooter's mind
items.description: Attacks like last week's school shooting in Turkey can seem to come out of nowhere, but they rarely do. Instead, a buildup of grievance and fixation often precedes the the moment violent thoughts become violent acts.

items.title: China praises blocking of Taiwan President's Africa trip, Taipei defiant
items.description: It is the first instance of a Taiwan President having to cancel an entire foreign trip due to denial of airspace access, representing a new Chinese strategy as its steps up ‌efforts to stifle the island’s efforts to engage internationally

items.title: Mapping the environmental toll of the Middle East war
items.description: Oil refineries set ablaze, missiles hitting pesticide factories, an oil slick in the Strait of Hormuz... In under two months, the US and Israel’s war on Iran has inflicted a heavy toll on the environment, fuelling concerns over the public health impact on local populations.

items.description: President Donald Trump on April 21 accused Iran of repeatedly violating the ceasefire with the U.S. in the war in West Asia

items.description: Manchester City relegate Burnley with 1-0 win to also take top spot from Arsenal in the Premier League title race.

items.title: Nearly 8,000 migrants dead or missing in 2025
items.description: Nearly 8,000 people died or disappeared on migration routes in 2025, according to the United Nations, a stark reminder that for many, the journey in search of safety or opportunity remains deadly.

items.title: Nigeria's Tinubu sacks finance minister in surprise cabinet reshuffle
items.description: Nigeria's President Bola Tinubu has sacked his finance minister Wale Edun as part of a minor cabinet reshuffle. Stepping into the role is former Minister of State for Finance Taiwo Oyedele. He played a central role in the administration's tax overhaul which took effect at the start of this year.

items.description: Allegations of a coup plot first surfaced last year when an Independence Day parade was cancelled.

items.description: Rat poison has been found in HiPP baby food jars across Austria, Czechia and Slovakia in what authorities suspect is a blackmail attempt.

items.description: An average cut of $7,000 to NDIS participant plans could hurt social supports providing the "only interaction with the outside world" for some people with disability, advocates warn, as the minister Mark Butler concedes the savings will have a material impact on participants.

items.title: No more green bin pick-ups for some residents amid fuel uncertainty
items.description: The Upper Lachlan Shire Council says it cannot afford to be caught off-guard if a diesel shipment does not arrive, and must reserve fuel for essential services like road grading or red bin collections.

items.description: US envoy Steve Witkoff and Donald Trump's son-in-law Jared Kushner have travelled to Moscow several times, but never to Kyiv.

items.description: Prosecutor General Ruslan Kravchenko detailed the previously unreported Russian military activity near Ukrainian nuclear sites in written ​remarks, as Ukraine prepares to mark on April 26, 40th anniversary of the 1986 Chornobyl disaster

items.description: The Russian army is advancing on the Ukrainian town of Kostiantynivka, with the aim of occupying the entire Donbas region. Yet people continue to live and fight in the area, as DW's Hanna Sokolova-Stekh found out.

items.title: Actor Luis Brandoni dies aged 86, nine days after suffering fall

items.description: European Union ambassadors unblocked ⁠on Wednesday a ​90-billion-euro ($106 billion) loan to Ukraine that is critical for Kyiv's ability to keep up the fight against Russia. The loan, which ​was approved by the European Council in December, had been halted by a Hungarian veto amid a dispute over oil flows via the Druzhba pipeline.

items.title: Nigeria faces jet fuel shortage as Strait of Hormuz disruption bites
items.description: Nigeria’s jet fuel shortage is being driven by disruptions at the Strait of Hormuz that have curtailed global supplies of middle distillates, a fuel marketers’ group said, squeezing airlines already grappling with soaring costs. Products such as jet fuel and diesel that normally pass through the strategic waterway have become less available worldwide, pushing up prices, the Major Energies Marketers Association of Nigeria (MEMAN) said.

items.description: NASA's Curiosity rover has identified more organic compounds as scientists investigate any potential ancient life signs on Mars. It was exploring the massive Gale Crater also believed to be a now-dry lake.

items.description: As Pakistan works to broker peace between the U.S. and Iran, many Pakistanis are watching closely to see whether the diplomatic boost will translate into improvements at home.

items.description: The world faces a "perilous new era" driven by states' "predatory" behaviour leading to an "anti-rights" international order, rights group Amnesty International warned on Tuesday.

items.title: U.S.-Iran ceasefire nears its end. And, Fed chair nominee faces tough hearing
items.description: The ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran is set to expire tomorrow. Peace talks between the countries remain uncertain. And, Trump's pick to lead the Federal Reserve faces a tough confirmation hearing today.

items.title: Jackson biopic 'Michael' is a family affair as nephew takes lead role
items.description: The premiere of the long-awaited Michael Jackson biopic 'Michael' brought emotion and reflection to Los Angeles on Monday, as cast members and the Jackson family shared the depth of effort behind bringing the King of Pop’s story to life.

items.title: Israeli ambassador: 'Only a strong Israel can achieve peace'
items.description: In an interview with DW, Ron Prosor, Israel's ambassador to Germany, defends his country's military strikes against Iran and Lebanon. He also advises Germany's Chancellor Friedrich Merz to show more "tact."

items.title: Germany news: Lufthansa scraps 20,000 flights
items.description: As Frankfurt Airport opens a new terminal, Lufthansa has scrapped 20,000 flights due to soaring fuel prices. Meanwhile, the Economy Ministry has reduced its forecast growth down to 0.5%. DW has more.

items.title: EU resumes aid to Ethiopia suspended over Tigray war
items.description: The European Union has said it will resume budgetary aid to Ethiopia, which was suspended when a brutal civil war broke out in 2020. The EU suspended its annual budgetary support payment in December 2020, a month after the start of the war between government forces and rebels from the northern Tigray region, which continued for two years and claimed some 600,000 lives. FRANCE 24's Tom Canetti reports from Addis Ababa, Ethiopia.

items.title: Lufthansa cuts 20,000 summer flights as fuel prices surge
items.description: The airline is the latest to cut flights as the US-Israel war with Iran sends jet fuel prices soaring.

items.description: A global shipping route is once again under threat as Iranian forces move against commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.description: U.S. President expressed surprise that the nation needs assistance, but made clear he was open to the prospect of making the move to help meet his ally's concerns

items.title: The rise and fall of East Germany's Palace of the Republic
items.description: From 1976 through 1990, the building in East Berlin served as a symbol of the communist state's power. The "People's Palace" was controversially destroyed — and yet remains present.

items.title: UK Starmer says unaware of security lapse in Mandelson security vetting
items.description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer was grilled during questions in Parliament over his decision to appoint Peter Mandelson as Britain's top diplomat to Washington, effectively brushing aside security concerns. Starmer once again faced calls to resign from the opposition, after former top civil servant Olly Robbins testified this week that there was an "atmosphere of pressure" from Downing Street to approve Mandelson's appointment. FRANCE 24's Bénédicte Paviot reports from London.

items.title: U.K. agrees ban on cigarette sales for people born after 2008
items.description: Health Minister Wes Streeting called this a "historic moment for the nation's health" that will lead to the "first smoke-free generation, protected from a lifetime of addiction and harm".

items.title: China's chip ambitions shake up global tech industry
items.description: US curbs on advanced chips pushed China to focus on its own semiconductor ecosystem. And while it trails at the very cutting edge, China's "good‑enough" technology is fast powering much of the global economy.

items.title: Taiwan president postpones Eswatini trip after claims of China pressure

items.title: 'Right now both sides are testing each other', analyst says as Iran truce extended
items.description: FRANCE 24's Mark Owen speaks with Negah Angha, Former National Security Advisor under US President Joe Biden, about the ongoing standoff between Iran and the United States, which has choked off all traffic through the Strait of Hormuz. She says that "right now both sides are testing each other" adding that "Iran has been articulating to the US and Western allies the impact and the control that they have on the global economy".
